notes: The engineering layer makes a CRITICAL choice: it adopts Shankara's reading of 'yoge' (karma-yoga as parallel discipline to jñāna, both required, neither reducible) and is HONEST in the counter-example about not preserving Ramanuja's sequential reading or Vishvanatha's bhakti reading at the same level. This kind of honest disclosure of which commentarial position the engineering layer is anchored to is exactly what cross-tradition fidelity requires. The three readings are all named in the source pack with their respective traditions (Advaita, Vishishtadvaita, Gaudiya Sampradaya). The engineering layer does not silently flatten the three into one; it adopts one explicitly and discloses the cost. The META function of the verse (Krishna naming the structure of his teaching) is preserved as the engineering staff naming the structure of the team's work — preserving the meta-level distinction without conflating the original Sanskrit term-act with the engineering act. PASS with high confidence.
